behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is at real `git` diff/status semantics plus CLI parsing. Rename detection is produced by `git` (similarity thresholds, status codes like `R100`) and can differ from mocked fixtures; a mock can hide misclassification where real subprocess output maps renames incorrectly to add+delete. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no AI/ML decision dependency, and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit baseline file, rename it, run the CLI against actual repo state via real `git` subprocess calls, and assert report shows count in `renamed` only (not `added`/`deleted`).

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Real failure depends on actual binary file handling in git diff output and subprocess behavior (binary markers, encoding, exit behavior). In-process stubs can miss parser crashes triggered by real command output and byte patterns. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output quality dependency; no chained agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, add/modify a true binary file (e.g., random bytes), run CLI, assert process exits successfully and report includes binary file in appropriate category/count.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real git unborn-HEAD behavior (error codes/messages for repos without commits) and how the CLI handles subprocess failures/edge responses. Mocking git responses can hide real failure modes in startup/exit-path handling. Rejected higher levels: behavior is deterministic infrastructure handling, not AI/ML; no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real empty git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert zero/empty structured report on stdout and successful/non-error exit behavior per spec.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: The claim is end-to-end correctness against actual repository history, requiring real commit/index/worktree and real git query behavior. Module-only tests can validate counting logic but may miss failures from real command output, ordering, rename detection, or history traversal nuances. Rejected higher levels: no model judgment involved; no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known added/deleted/renamed/modified events across commits, run CLI, and assert each reported count equals the ground-truth operations performed.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git index vs working-tree semantics, which are OS+subprocess-backed and often diverge in subtle ways. A fake repo layer can mask discrepancies in how staged/unstaged deltas are computed from real git commands. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependency and no multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, produce equivalent content changes once as unstaged and once staged; run CLI in both states and assert structured stdout is byte-for-byte identical (or structurally identical if ordering normalization is specified).
